questions.prompt: In a 3×3 matrix, a triangle rotates 90° clockwise across each row. The second row starts at 180°. What orientation should appear in row 2, column 3?
questions.hint: Track the rotation: each cell adds 90° to the previous one in the same row.

questions.prompt: Shape A to B adds one side (triangle → square). Shape C is a pentagon. What is the result of the same transformation?
questions.rationale: Adding one side to a pentagon gives a hexagon.

questions.prompt: A cube has opposite faces colored: red–blue, green–yellow, black–white. If red is on top and green faces you, which color is on the bottom?
questions.rationale: Red is opposite blue by definition, so blue is on the bottom.

questions.prompt: Each figure in the sequence adds one dot to the previous pattern, following a spiral path. The first has 1 dot (center), the second has 2 (center + right), the third has 3 (center + right + top-right). How many dots are in the 7th figure?
questions.rationale: Each figure simply adds one dot: the nth figure has n dots.

questions.prompt: You fold a square piece of paper in half diagonally, then cut a small circle at the folded corner. When you unfold the paper, how many holes do you see?
questions.rationale: Cutting at the folded corner affects only the single corner point. When unfolded, there is 1 hole at the center of the original square.

questions.prompt: You are looking at a clock in a mirror. The mirror shows the time as 2:50. What is the actual time?
questions.rationale: Mirror reflection of a clock: subtract from 12:00. 12:00 - 2:50 = 9:10.

questions.prompt: Each step doubles the number of sides in the shape. Step 1: triangle (3), Step 2: hexagon (6), Step 3: ?

A 3×3 grid contains shapes. Each row has a circle, a square, and a triangle. Each column has a small, medium, and large shape. The center cell is a medium square. What must be in the top-right cell if the top-left is a small circle?
questions.hint: Each row and column must have all three shapes and all three sizes.
questions.rationale: Top row has small circle, so needs square and triangle. Center row has medium square in center. Top row must have medium or large for remaining. By elimination, top-right is large triangle.

questions.prompt: A shape with exactly 4 sides, where all sides are equal and all angles are 90°, must be a square.
questions.rationale: These are the exact defining properties of a square.
